1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Bash Utility Handbook - Always-visible user interaction output
20	#
21	# This module provides formatted output for interactive procedures where
22	# the user MUST see the output to proceed (OAuth flows, manual steps, etc).
23	# All output goes to stderr and is NOT subject to verbosity control.
24	#
25	# Function naming convention encodes color sequence for positional args:
26	#   t = text (default color)
27	#   c = command/code (cyan)
28	#   u = UI element (magenta) - text user sees on screen
29	#   W = warning (yellow) - uppercase for visual pop
30	#   E = error (red) - uppercase for visual pop
31	#   l = link (blue underline, OSC-8 hyperlink) - consumes TWO args
32	#
33	# Each letter consumes one positional argument, except 'l' which consumes
34	# two (display_text then url). This is the only multi-arg letter.
35	#
36	# Example: buh_tut "Click the " "Save" " button"
37	#   Renders: "Click the " + magenta("Save") + " button"
38	#
39	# Link arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
40	#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
41	#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
42	#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)
43	
44	set -euo pipefail
45	
46	# Multiple inclusion guard
47	test -z "${ZBUH_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
48	ZBUH_INCLUDED=1
49	
50	######################################################################
51	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
52	
53	zbuh_kindle() {
54	  test -z "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" || return 0
55	
56	  # Color support detection
57	  local z_use_color=0
58	  if test -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
59	    z_use_color=1
60	  fi
61	
62	  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1"; then
63	    readonly ZBUH_R="\033[0m"          # Reset
64	    readonly ZBUH_T=""                 # Text (default - no color change)
65	    readonly ZBUH_C="\033[36m"         # Command (cyan)
66	    readonly ZBUH_U="\033[35m"         # UI element (magenta)
67	    readonly ZBUH_W="\033[1;33m"       # Warning (bright yellow)
68	    readonly ZBUH_E="\033[1;31m"       # Error (bright red)
69	    readonly ZBUH_S="\033[1;37m"       # Section (bright white)
70	    readonly ZBUH_L="\033[34;4m"       # Link (blue + underline)
71	  else
72	    readonly ZBUH_R=""
73	    readonly ZBUH_T=""
74	    readonly ZBUH_C=""
75	    readonly ZBUH_U=""
76	    readonly ZBUH_W=""
77	    readonly ZBUH_E=""
78	    readonly ZBUH_S=""
79	    readonly ZBUH_L=""
80	  fi
81	
82	  # Mutable kindle state — step counters, body indent, step format
83	  z_buh_step1_n=0
84	  z_buh_step2_n=0
85	  z_buh_body_indent=""
86	  z_buh_step_prefix=""
87	  z_buh_step_separator=". "
88	
89	  readonly ZBUH_KINDLED=1
90	}
91	
92	zbuh_sentinel() {
93	  test "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || { zbuh_kindle; }
94	}
95	
96	######################################################################
97	# Internal: Core output
98	
99	zbuh_show() {
100	  zbuh_sentinel
101	  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${1:-}" >&2
102	}
103	
104	######################################################################
105	# Internal: Link fragment builder
106	#
107	# Sets ZBUH_LINK_FRAG to a formatted link fragment for embedding in
108	# combinator output.  Respects BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS fallback.
109	# Args: display_text url
110	
111	zbuh_link_fragment() {
112	  zbuh_sentinel
113	  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
114	  local -r z_url="${2:-}"
115	  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
116	    ZBUH_LINK_FRAG="${ZBUH_L}${z_text}${ZBUH_R} <${z_url}>"
117	  else
118	    ZBUH_LINK_FRAG="${ZBUH_L}\033]8;;${z_url}\033\\\\${z_text}\033]8;;\033\\\\${ZBUH_R}"
119	  fi
120	}
121	
122	######################################################################
123	# Public: Section headers and numbered steps
124	
125	buh_section() { zbuh_sentinel; z_buh_body_indent=""; printf '%b\n' "${ZBUH_S}${1}${ZBUH_R}" >&2; }
126	buh_e()       { echo "" >&2; }
127	
128	buh_step_style() {
129	  zbuh_sentinel
130	  z_buh_step_prefix="${1:-}"
131	  z_buh_step_separator="${2:-". "}"
132	}
133	
134	buh_step1() {
135	  zbuh_sentinel
136	  z_buh_step1_n=$((z_buh_step1_n + 1))
137	  z_buh_step2_n=0
138	  z_buh_body_indent="   "
139	  printf '%b\n' "${ZBUH_S}${z_buh_step_prefix}${z_buh_step1_n}${z_buh_step_separator}${1}${ZBUH_R}" >&2
140	}
141	
142	buh_step2() {
143	  zbuh_sentinel
144	  z_buh_step2_n=$((z_buh_step2_n + 1))
145	  z_buh_body_indent="      "
146	  printf '%b\n' "   ${ZBUH_S}${z_buh_step_prefix}${z_buh_step1_n}.${z_buh_step2_n}${z_buh_step_separator}${1}${ZBUH_R}" >&2
147	}
148	
149	######################################################################
150	# Public: Text combinators
151	#
152	# Naming: sequence of t/c/u/W/E describes positional arg colors
153	# Each letter consumes one positional argument
154	
155	# Single element (sorted)
156	buh_c()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
157	buh_E()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
158	buh_t()       { zbuh_show "${1}"; }
159	buh_u()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_U}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
160	buh_W()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_W}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
161	
162	# Two elements (sorted)
163	buh_ct()      { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}${2}"; }
164	buh_tc()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
165	buh_tE()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_E}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
166	buh_tu()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
167	buh_tW()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_W}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
168	buh_ut()      { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_U}${1}${ZBUH_R}${2}"; }
169	
170	# Three elements (sorted)
171	buh_tct()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
172	buh_tcu()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_U}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
173	buh_tEt()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_E}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
174	buh_tuc()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
175	buh_tut()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
176	buh_tWt()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_W}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
177	
178	# Four elements (sorted)
179	buh_tctc()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
180	buh_tctu()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
181	buh_tcut()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_U}${3}${ZBUH_R}${4}"; }
182	buh_tuct()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}${4}"; }
183	buh_tutE()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_E}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
184	buh_tutu()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
185	
186	# Five elements (sorted)
187	buh_tctct()   { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}${5}"; }
188	buh_tutut()   { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}${5}"; }
189	
190	######################################################################
191	# Public: Hyperlinks
192	#
193	# buh_link "prefix text" "link text" "url" "suffix text"
194	# Renders clickable hyperlink with OSC-8 escape sequences
195	
196	buh_link() {
197	  zbuh_sentinel
198	  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
199	  local z_text="${2:-}"
200	  local z_url="${3:-}"
201	  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
202	
203	  # Blue + underline style
204	  local z_link_style="\033[34;4m"
205	
206	  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
207	    # Fallback: styled text with URL in angle brackets
208	    printf '%s%s%b%s%b <%s>%s\n' \
209	      "${z_buh_body_indent}" "${z_prefix}" "${z_link_style}" "${z_text}" "${ZBUH_R}" "${z_url}" "${z_suffix}" >&2
210	  else
211	    # OSC-8 hyperlink with styling
212	    printf '%s%s%b\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%b%s\n' \
213	      "${z_buh_body_indent}" "${z_prefix}" "${z_link_style}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${ZBUH_R}" "${z_suffix}" >&2
214	  fi
215	}
216	
217	######################################################################
218	# Public: Link combinators
219	#
220	# The 'l' letter consumes TWO positional args (display_text, url) and
221	# renders an OSC-8 terminal hyperlink.  All other letters consume one.
222	#
223	# Arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
224	#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
225	#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
226	#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)
227	
228	buh_lt()      { zbuh_link_fragment "${1}" "${2}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${3}"; }
229	buh_tl()      { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"; }
230	buh_tlt()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${4}"; }
231	buh_tlc()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
232	
233	buh_tltlt() {
234	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
235	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
236	  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
237	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${7}"
238	}
239	
240	buh_tltltlt() {
241	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
242	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
243	  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
244	  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
245	  zbuh_link_fragment "${8}" "${9}"
246	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${7}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${10}"
247	}
248	
249	buh_tltltltlt() {
250	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
251	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
252	  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
253	  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
254	  zbuh_link_fragment "${8}" "${9}"
255	  local -r z_frag3="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
256	  zbuh_link_fragment "${11}" "${12}"
257	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${7}${z_frag3}${10}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${13}"
258	}
259	
260	buh_tltTtT() {
261	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
262	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
263	  zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${5}"
264	  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"
265	  zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${7}"
266	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${6}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${8}"
267	}
268	
269	######################################################################
270	# Internal: Tabtarget fragment builder
271	#
272	# Sets ZBUH_TT_FRAG to a resolved tabtarget path in cyan for embedding
273	# in combinator output.  Resolves colophon via glob.
274	# Args: colophon
275	
276	zbuh_tabtarget_fragment() {
277	  zbuh_sentinel
278	  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${1}.*)
279	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buh: no tabtarget for colophon '${1}'"
280	  ZBUH_TT_FRAG="${ZBUH_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUH_R}"
281	}
282	
283	# Imprint tabtarget fragment: resolves colophon + imprint to a specific
284	# imprint tabtarget.  Glob: ${colophon}.*.${imprint}.sh
285	# Args: colophon imprint
286	
287	zbuh_imprint_fragment() {
288	  zbuh_sentinel
289	  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${1}.*.${2}.sh)
290	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buh: no tabtarget for colophon '${1}' imprint '${2}'"
291	  ZBUH_TT_FRAG="${ZBUH_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUH_R}"
292	}
293	
294	######################################################################
295	# Public: Tabtarget combinators
296	#
297	# T consumes one positional arg (colophon), resolves to tabtarget path,
298	# renders in cyan.  Parallel to l (link) combinator.
299	# I consumes two positional args (colophon, imprint), resolves to a
300	# specific imprint tabtarget.
301	# Trailing c after T or I appends cyan text (arguments) to the resolved
302	# path, keeping the whole command visually unified.
303	
304	buh_T()       { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${1}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
305	buh_tT()      { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
306	buh_tTc()     { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
307	buh_tI()      { zbuh_imprint_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
308	buh_tIc()     { zbuh_imprint_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
309	buh_cT()      { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
310	buh_tlT()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${4}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG} ${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
311	buh_tltT()    { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${5}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${4}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
312	
313	######################################################################
314	# Public: Index renderers
315	#
316	# Group-level handbook indexes shared across kits.
317	# Each function renders one kit's handbook group tops.
318	
319	buh_index_buk() {
320	  buh_section  "Generic OS Procedures (Bash Utility Kit)"
321	  buh_t        "  Project-independent OS-level mechanisms."
322	  buh_tT       "  Windows:  " "buw-hw"
323	}
324	
325	######################################################################
326	# Public: User prompts
327	
328	# buh_prompt "prompt text"
329	# Displays prompt and reads user input, returns via stdout
330	buh_prompt() {
331	  zbuh_sentinel
332	  printf '%s' "${1:-}" >&2
333	  local z_input
334	  read -r z_input
335	  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
336	}
337	
338	# buh_prompt_required "prompt text" "error message"
339	# Like buh_prompt but dies if input is empty
340	buh_prompt_required() {
341	  local z_input
342	  z_input=$(buh_prompt "${1:-}")
343	  if test -z "${z_input}"; then
344	    zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}ERROR:${ZBUH_R} ${2:-Input required}"
345	    return 1
346	  fi
347	  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
348	}
349	
350	######################################################################
351	# Public: Critical warnings (box format)
352	
353	buh_critical() {
354	  zbuh_sentinel
355	  zbuh_show ""
356	  zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}===============================================${ZBUH_R}"
357	  zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}  CRITICAL: ${1}${ZBUH_R}"
358	  zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}===============================================${ZBUH_R}"
359	  zbuh_show ""
360	}
361	
362	# eof
363

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Always-visible user interaction output
#
# This module provides formatted output for interactive procedures where
# the user MUST see the output to proceed (OAuth flows, manual steps, etc).
# All output goes to stderr and is NOT subject to verbosity control.
#
# Function naming convention encodes color sequence for positional args:
#   t = text (default color)
#   c = command/code (cyan)
#   u = UI element (magenta) - text user sees on screen
#   W = warning (yellow) - uppercase for visual pop
#   E = error (red) - uppercase for visual pop
#   l = link (blue underline, OSC-8 hyperlink) - consumes TWO args
#
# Each letter consumes one positional argument, except 'l' which consumes
# two (display_text then url). This is the only multi-arg letter.
#
# Example: buh_tut "Click the " "Save" " button"
#   Renders: "Click the " + magenta("Save") + " button"
#
# Link arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUH_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUH_INCLUDED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" || return 0

  # Color support detection
  local z_use_color=0
  if test -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
    z_use_color=1
  fi

  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1"; then
    readonly ZBUH_R="\033[0m"          # Reset
    readonly ZBUH_T=""                 # Text (default - no color change)
    readonly ZBUH_C="\033[36m"         # Command (cyan)
    readonly ZBUH_U="\033[35m"         # UI element (magenta)
    readonly ZBUH_W="\033[1;33m"       # Warning (bright yellow)
    readonly ZBUH_E="\033[1;31m"       # Error (bright red)
    readonly ZBUH_S="\033[1;37m"       # Section (bright white)
    readonly ZBUH_L="\033[34;4m"       # Link (blue + underline)
  else
    readonly ZBUH_R=""
    readonly ZBUH_T=""
    readonly ZBUH_C=""
    readonly ZBUH_U=""
    readonly ZBUH_W=""
    readonly ZBUH_E=""
    readonly ZBUH_S=""
    readonly ZBUH_L=""
  fi

  # Mutable kindle state — step counters, body indent, step format
  z_buh_step1_n=0
  z_buh_step2_n=0
  z_buh_body_indent=""
  z_buh_step_prefix=""
  z_buh_step_separator=". "

  readonly ZBUH_KINDLED=1
}

zbuh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || { zbuh_kindle; }
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Core output

zbuh_show() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${1:-}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Link fragment builder
#
# Sets ZBUH_LINK_FRAG to a formatted link fragment for embedding in
# combinator output.  Respects BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS fallback.
# Args: display_text url

zbuh_link_fragment() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
  local -r z_url="${2:-}"
  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    ZBUH_LINK_FRAG="${ZBUH_L}${z_text}${ZBUH_R} <${z_url}>"
  else
    ZBUH_LINK_FRAG="${ZBUH_L}\033]8;;${z_url}\033\\\\${z_text}\033]8;;\033\\\\${ZBUH_R}"
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Public: Section headers and numbered steps

buh_section() { zbuh_sentinel; z_buh_body_indent=""; printf '%b\n' "${ZBUH_S}${1}${ZBUH_R}" >&2; }
buh_e()       { echo "" >&2; }

buh_step_style() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  z_buh_step_prefix="${1:-}"
  z_buh_step_separator="${2:-". "}"
}

buh_step1() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  z_buh_step1_n=$((z_buh_step1_n + 1))
  z_buh_step2_n=0
  z_buh_body_indent="   "
  printf '%b\n' "${ZBUH_S}${z_buh_step_prefix}${z_buh_step1_n}${z_buh_step_separator}${1}${ZBUH_R}" >&2
}

buh_step2() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  z_buh_step2_n=$((z_buh_step2_n + 1))
  z_buh_body_indent="      "
  printf '%b\n' "   ${ZBUH_S}${z_buh_step_prefix}${z_buh_step1_n}.${z_buh_step2_n}${z_buh_step_separator}${1}${ZBUH_R}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Public: Text combinators
#
# Naming: sequence of t/c/u/W/E describes positional arg colors
# Each letter consumes one positional argument

# Single element (sorted)
buh_c()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_E()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_t()       { zbuh_show "${1}"; }
buh_u()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_U}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_W()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_W}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }

# Two elements (sorted)
buh_ct()      { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}${2}"; }
buh_tc()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tE()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_E}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tu()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tW()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_W}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_ut()      { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_U}${1}${ZBUH_R}${2}"; }

# Three elements (sorted)
buh_tct()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
buh_tcu()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_U}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tEt()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_E}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
buh_tuc()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tut()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
buh_tWt()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_W}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }

# Four elements (sorted)
buh_tctc()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tctu()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tcut()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_U}${3}${ZBUH_R}${4}"; }
buh_tuct()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}${4}"; }
buh_tutE()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_E}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tutu()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }

# Five elements (sorted)
buh_tctct()   { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}${5}"; }
buh_tutut()   { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}${5}"; }

######################################################################
# Public: Hyperlinks
#
# buh_link "prefix text" "link text" "url" "suffix text"
# Renders clickable hyperlink with OSC-8 escape sequences

buh_link() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
  local z_text="${2:-}"
  local z_url="${3:-}"
  local z_suffix="${4:-}"

  # Blue + underline style
  local z_link_style="\033[34;4m"

  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    # Fallback: styled text with URL in angle brackets
    printf '%s%s%b%s%b <%s>%s\n' \
      "${z_buh_body_indent}" "${z_prefix}" "${z_link_style}" "${z_text}" "${ZBUH_R}" "${z_url}" "${z_suffix}" >&2
  else
    # OSC-8 hyperlink with styling
    printf '%s%s%b\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%b%s\n' \
      "${z_buh_body_indent}" "${z_prefix}" "${z_link_style}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${ZBUH_R}" "${z_suffix}" >&2
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Public: Link combinators
#
# The 'l' letter consumes TWO positional args (display_text, url) and
# renders an OSC-8 terminal hyperlink.  All other letters consume one.
#
# Arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)

buh_lt()      { zbuh_link_fragment "${1}" "${2}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${3}"; }
buh_tl()      { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"; }
buh_tlt()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${4}"; }
buh_tlc()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }

buh_tltlt() {
  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${7}"
}

buh_tltltlt() {
  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_link_fragment "${8}" "${9}"
  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${7}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${10}"
}

buh_tltltltlt() {
  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_link_fragment "${8}" "${9}"
  local -r z_frag3="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_link_fragment "${11}" "${12}"
  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${7}${z_frag3}${10}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${13}"
}

buh_tltTtT() {
  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
  zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${5}"
  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"
  zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${7}"
  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${6}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${8}"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Tabtarget fragment builder
#
# Sets ZBUH_TT_FRAG to a resolved tabtarget path in cyan for embedding
# in combinator output.  Resolves colophon via glob.
# Args: colophon

zbuh_tabtarget_fragment() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${1}.*)
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buh: no tabtarget for colophon '${1}'"
  ZBUH_TT_FRAG="${ZBUH_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUH_R}"
}

# Imprint tabtarget fragment: resolves colophon + imprint to a specific
# imprint tabtarget.  Glob: ${colophon}.*.${imprint}.sh
# Args: colophon imprint

zbuh_imprint_fragment() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${1}.*.${2}.sh)
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buh: no tabtarget for colophon '${1}' imprint '${2}'"
  ZBUH_TT_FRAG="${ZBUH_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUH_R}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Tabtarget combinators
#
# T consumes one positional arg (colophon), resolves to tabtarget path,
# renders in cyan.  Parallel to l (link) combinator.
# I consumes two positional args (colophon, imprint), resolves to a
# specific imprint tabtarget.
# Trailing c after T or I appends cyan text (arguments) to the resolved
# path, keeping the whole command visually unified.

buh_T()       { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${1}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
buh_tT()      { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
buh_tTc()     { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_tI()      { zbuh_imprint_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
buh_tIc()     { zbuh_imprint_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
buh_cT()      { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
buh_tlT()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${4}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG} ${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
buh_tltT()    { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${5}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${4}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }

######################################################################
# Public: Index renderers
#
# Group-level handbook indexes shared across kits.
# Each function renders one kit's handbook group tops.

buh_index_buk() {
  buh_section  "Generic OS Procedures (Bash Utility Kit)"
  buh_t        "  Project-independent OS-level mechanisms."
  buh_tT       "  Windows:  " "buw-hw"
}

######################################################################
# Public: User prompts

# buh_prompt "prompt text"
# Displays prompt and reads user input, returns via stdout
buh_prompt() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  printf '%s' "${1:-}" >&2
  local z_input
  read -r z_input
  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
}

# buh_prompt_required "prompt text" "error message"
# Like buh_prompt but dies if input is empty
buh_prompt_required() {
  local z_input
  z_input=$(buh_prompt "${1:-}")
  if test -z "${z_input}"; then
    zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}ERROR:${ZBUH_R} ${2:-Input required}"
    return 1
  fi
  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Critical warnings (box format)

buh_critical() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  zbuh_show ""
  zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}===============================================${ZBUH_R}"
  zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}  CRITICAL: ${1}${ZBUH_R}"
  zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}===============================================${ZBUH_R}"
  zbuh_show ""
}

# eof

# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Nameplate - ccyolo
# Claude Code sandbox for network-contained AI development

# Core Service Definition
RBRN_MONIKER=ccyolo
RBRN_DESCRIPTION="Claude Code sandbox with SSH entry, Anthropic-only network access"
RBRN_RUNTIME=docker

# Vessel Source Configuration (GAR vessels)
RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL=rbev-sentry-debian-slim
RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=rbev-bottle-ccyolo
RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=k260413155458-f9dcb6d9
RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=k260413161216-99e62002

# Entry Service Configuration (SSH from workstation into bottle)
RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=enabled
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=2222
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=22

# Network Uplink Configuration
RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN=10000
RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE=allowlist
RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE=allowlist
# example.com is a test target for verifying network containment — proves
# allowlist works for a non-Anthropic domain while everything else stays
# blocked. Currently behind Cloudflare (104.16.0.0/12, 172.64.0.0/13).
# Remove once containment is validated.
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS="160.79.104.0/23 104.16.0.0/12 172.64.0.0/13"
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS="api.anthropic.com claude.ai example.com"

# Network Configuration
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP=10.242.3.0
RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK=24
RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP=10.242.3.2
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP=10.242.3.3

1	# ccyolo — Claude Code sandbox bottle for Recipe Bottle crucible
2	#
3	# SSH-accessible container running Claude Code. The sentry DNAT
4	# forwards ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION to this container's port 22.
5	# User SSHs in as "claude" (empty password, PAM-permissive),
6	# then runs `claude` to authenticate via OAuth copy/paste flow.
7	#
8	# Build context: rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/ (co-located with Dockerfile)
9	
10	ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
11	FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}
12	
13	# Avoid prompts from apt
14	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
15	
16	# Install SSH server and Claude Code operational dependencies
17	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
18	    openssh-server    `# SSH daemon for workstation entry via sentry DNAT`  \
19	    ca-certificates   `# TLS root certs — required for HTTPS to Anthropic API` \
20	    curl              `# HTTP client (Claude Code operational dependency)`  \
21	    git               `# Version control (Claude Code operational dependency)` \
22	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
23	
24	# Install Claude Code globally via npm (node and npm provided by base image)
25	RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
26	
27	# Create unprivileged claude user with empty password
28	RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash claude && \
29	    passwd -d claude
30	
31	# Generate SSH host keys
32	RUN ssh-keygen -A
33	
34	# Configure SSH daemon
35	RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd                                       && \
36	    echo "Port 22"                    >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
37	    echo "PermitRootLogin no"         >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
38	    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords yes"   >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
39	    echo "PasswordAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
40	
41	# Configure PAM for permissive authentication (no password prompt)
42	RUN sed -i 's/@include common-auth/#@include common-auth/' /etc/pam.d/sshd && \
43	    echo "auth sufficient pam_permit.so" >> /etc/pam.d/sshd
44	
45	# Ensure PATH includes npm global bin for SSH sessions
46	RUN echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc
47	
48	COPY entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
49	RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
50	
51	EXPOSE 22
52	
53	USER root
54	ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh"]
55

file.content:
# ccyolo — Claude Code sandbox bottle for Recipe Bottle crucible
#
# SSH-accessible container running Claude Code. The sentry DNAT
# forwards ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION to this container's port 22.
# User SSHs in as "claude" (empty password, PAM-permissive),
# then runs `claude` to authenticate via OAuth copy/paste flow.
#
# Build context: rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/ (co-located with Dockerfile)

ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}

# Avoid prompts from apt
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install SSH server and Claude Code operational dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    openssh-server    `# SSH daemon for workstation entry via sentry DNAT`  \
    ca-certificates   `# TLS root certs — required for HTTPS to Anthropic API` \
    curl              `# HTTP client (Claude Code operational dependency)`  \
    git               `# Version control (Claude Code operational dependency)` \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Claude Code globally via npm (node and npm provided by base image)
RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# Create unprivileged claude user with empty password
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash claude && \
    passwd -d claude

# Generate SSH host keys
RUN ssh-keygen -A

# Configure SSH daemon
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd                                       && \
    echo "Port 22"                    >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PermitRootLogin no"         >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords yes"   >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PasswordAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Configure PAM for permissive authentication (no password prompt)
RUN sed -i 's/@include common-auth/#@include common-auth/' /etc/pam.d/sshd && \
    echo "auth sufficient pam_permit.so" >> /etc/pam.d/sshd

# Ensure PATH includes npm global bin for SSH sessions
RUN echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc

COPY entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 22

USER root
ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh"]

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Handbook - Cross-role Onboarding Walkthroughs
20	#
21	# Per-role onboarding tracks with dual-mode rendering (walkthrough when any
22	# probe red, reference when all green). Filesystem-only probes — functions
23	# work pre-kindle and require only the display infrastructure (buh_*) plus
24	# regime constants (rbcc, rbgc, rbz).
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	# Multiple inclusion detection
29	test -z "${ZRBHO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbho multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
30	ZRBHO_SOURCED=1
31	
32	######################################################################
33	# Module kindle — verifies prerequisite modules are kindled
34	#
35	# rbho walkthroughs do not require rbho-local state (they probe the
36	# filesystem directly and render via buh_*). The kindle exists to assert
37	# the dependency ordering is correct at furnish time.
38	
39	zrbho_kindle() {
40	  test -z "${ZRBHO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbho already kindled"
41	  zrbgc_sentinel
42	  zbuz_sentinel
43	  zrbz_sentinel
44	  readonly ZRBHO_KINDLED=1
45	}
46	
47	zrbho_sentinel() {
48	  test "${ZRBHO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbho not kindled - call zrbho_kindle first"
49	}
50	
51	######################################################################
52	# Probe utilities — no sentinels, all work pre-kindle
53	#
54	# These are filesystem probes for onboarding status. They set
55	# caller-scope variables; callers declare the variables locally first.
56	
57	# Dashboard status line
58	zrbho_po_status() {
59	  local -r z_flag="${1:-}"
60	  local -r z_text="${2:-}"
61	  if test "${z_flag}" = "1"; then
62	    buh_ct " [*] " "${z_text}"
63	  else
64	    buh_t " [ ] ${z_text}"
65	  fi
66	}
67	
68	# Extract a KEY=VALUE from a file; stdout empty if missing.  No sourcing.
69	zrbho_po_extract_capture() {
70	  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
71	  local -r z_key="${2:-}"
72	  test -n "${z_key}"  || return 1
73	  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
74	  local z_line=""
75	  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
76	    case "${z_line}" in "${z_key}="*) echo "${z_line#"${z_key}="}"; return 0 ;; esac
77	  done < "${z_file}"
78	  return 1
79	}
80	
81	######################################################################
82	# Shared credential probe — sets caller-scoped:
83	# z_has_payor, z_has_governor, z_has_director, z_has_retriever, z_secrets_dir
84	
85	zrbho_probe_role_credentials() {
86	  z_has_payor=0
87	  z_has_governor=0
88	  z_has_director=0
89	  z_has_retriever=0
90	  z_secrets_dir=""
91	
92	  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
93	    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
94	  fi
95	
96	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
97	    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/rbro-payor.env"                          && z_has_payor=1
98	    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"  && z_has_governor=1
99	    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"  && z_has_director=1
100	    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" && z_has_retriever=1
101	  fi
102	}
103	
104	######################################################################
105	# Probe retriever walkthrough units — sets caller-scope z_ru1..z_ru4
106	# Requires: z_secrets_dir already set (from zrbho_probe_role_credentials
107	#           or direct extraction).
108	
109	zrbho_probe_retriever_units() {
110	  z_ru1=0; z_ru2=0; z_ru3=0; z_ru4=0
111	
112	  # Unit 1: Retriever credential file exists
113	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
114	     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
115	    z_ru1=1
116	  fi
117	
118	  # Units 2-4 require Docker
119	  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then return 0; fi
120	
121	  # Unit 2: Any image from this depot's GAR exists locally
122	  local z_project_id="" z_region=""
123	  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
124	    z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID") || z_project_id=""
125	    z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
126	  fi
127	  if test -n "${z_region}" && test -n "${z_project_id}"; then
128	    local z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
129	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${z_gar_prefix}"; then
130	      z_ru2=1
131	    fi
132	  fi
133	
134	  # Unit 3: Any crucible is charged (bottle container running)
135	  if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q -- "-bottle$"; then
136	    z_ru3=1
137	  fi
138	
139	  # Unit 4: Kludge-tagged image exists (k-prefixed hallmark)
140	  if docker images --format "{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^k[0-9]"; then
141	    z_ru4=1
142	  fi
143	}
144	
145	######################################################################
146	# Probe director walkthrough units — sets caller-scope z_du1..z_du7
147	# Requires: z_secrets_dir already set.
148	#
149	# Vessel assignments per docket:
150	#   Unit 2,4,6: rbev-sentry-debian-slim (conjure vessel)
151	#   Unit 5:     rbev-bottle-plantuml    (bind vessel)
152	#
153	# Probes use local filesystem + docker only (no GAR API, no gcloud).
154	# Units 3-7 check docker image tags — these require the user to have
155	# summoned (pulled) the result locally after cloud operations.
156	# Unit 6 (graft) is detectable without summoning because graft tags
157	# the local image before pushing.
158	
159	zrbho_probe_director_units() {
160	  z_du1=0
161	  z_du2=0
162	  z_du3=0
163	  z_du4=0
164	  z_du5=0
165	  z_du6=0
166	  z_du7=0
167	
168	  # Unit 1: Director credential file exists
169	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
170	     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
171	    z_du1=1
172	  fi
173	
174	  # Units 2-7 require Docker
175	  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then return 0; fi
176	
177	  # Build GAR image prefix for this depot
178	  local z_project_id=""
179	  local z_region=""
180	  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
181	    z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID") || z_project_id=""
182	    z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
183	  fi
184	  if test -z "${z_region}" || test -z "${z_project_id}"; then return 0; fi
185	
186	  local -r z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
187	  local -r z_docker_images="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbho_probe_director_images.txt"
188	  local -r z_docker_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbho_probe_director_stderr.txt"
189	
190	  # Collect docker images into temp file
191	  docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
192	    > "${z_docker_images}" 2>"${z_docker_stderr}" \
193	    || return 0
194	
195	  # Load lines matching this depot's GAR prefix into array
196	  local z_depot_images=()
197	  local z_line=""
198	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
199	    case "${z_line}" in
200	      "${z_gar_prefix}"*) z_depot_images+=("${z_line}") ;;

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Handbook - Cross-role Onboarding Walkthroughs
#
# Per-role onboarding tracks with dual-mode rendering (walkthrough when any
# probe red, reference when all green). Filesystem-only probes — functions
# work pre-kindle and require only the display infrastructure (buh_*) plus
# regime constants (rbcc, rbgc, rbz).

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBHO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbho multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBHO_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Module kindle — verifies prerequisite modules are kindled
#
# rbho walkthroughs do not require rbho-local state (they probe the
# filesystem directly and render via buh_*). The kindle exists to assert
# the dependency ordering is correct at furnish time.

zrbho_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBHO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbho already kindled"
  zrbgc_sentinel
  zbuz_sentinel
  zrbz_sentinel
  readonly ZRBHO_KINDLED=1
}

zrbho_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBHO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbho not kindled - call zrbho_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Probe utilities — no sentinels, all work pre-kindle
#
# These are filesystem probes for onboarding status. They set
# caller-scope variables; callers declare the variables locally first.

# Dashboard status line
zrbho_po_status() {
  local -r z_flag="${1:-}"
  local -r z_text="${2:-}"
  if test "${z_flag}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct " [*] " "${z_text}"
  else
    buh_t " [ ] ${z_text}"
  fi
}

# Extract a KEY=VALUE from a file; stdout empty if missing.  No sourcing.
zrbho_po_extract_capture() {
  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_key="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_key}"  || return 1
  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    case "${z_line}" in "${z_key}="*) echo "${z_line#"${z_key}="}"; return 0 ;; esac
  done < "${z_file}"
  return 1
}

######################################################################
# Shared credential probe — sets caller-scoped:
# z_has_payor, z_has_governor, z_has_director, z_has_retriever, z_secrets_dir

zrbho_probe_role_credentials() {
  z_has_payor=0
  z_has_governor=0
  z_has_director=0
  z_has_retriever=0
  z_secrets_dir=""

  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi

  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/rbro-payor.env"                          && z_has_payor=1
    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"  && z_has_governor=1
    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"  && z_has_director=1
    test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" && z_has_retriever=1
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Probe retriever walkthrough units — sets caller-scope z_ru1..z_ru4
# Requires: z_secrets_dir already set (from zrbho_probe_role_credentials
#           or direct extraction).

zrbho_probe_retriever_units() {
  z_ru1=0; z_ru2=0; z_ru3=0; z_ru4=0

  # Unit 1: Retriever credential file exists
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_ru1=1
  fi

  # Units 2-4 require Docker
  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then return 0; fi

  # Unit 2: Any image from this depot's GAR exists locally
  local z_project_id="" z_region=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID") || z_project_id=""
    z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
  fi
  if test -n "${z_region}" && test -n "${z_project_id}"; then
    local z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${z_gar_prefix}"; then
      z_ru2=1
    fi
  fi

  # Unit 3: Any crucible is charged (bottle container running)
  if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q -- "-bottle$"; then
    z_ru3=1
  fi

  # Unit 4: Kludge-tagged image exists (k-prefixed hallmark)
  if docker images --format "{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^k[0-9]"; then
    z_ru4=1
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Probe director walkthrough units — sets caller-scope z_du1..z_du7
# Requires: z_secrets_dir already set.
#
# Vessel assignments per docket:
#   Unit 2,4,6: rbev-sentry-debian-slim (conjure vessel)
#   Unit 5:     rbev-bottle-plantuml    (bind vessel)
#
# Probes use local filesystem + docker only (no GAR API, no gcloud).
# Units 3-7 check docker image tags — these require the user to have
# summoned (pulled) the result locally after cloud operations.
# Unit 6 (graft) is detectable without summoning because graft tags
# the local image before pushing.

zrbho_probe_director_units() {
  z_du1=0
  z_du2=0
  z_du3=0
  z_du4=0
  z_du5=0
  z_du6=0
  z_du7=0

  # Unit 1: Director credential file exists
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_du1=1
  fi

  # Units 2-7 require Docker
  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then return 0; fi

  # Build GAR image prefix for this depot
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_region=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID") || z_project_id=""
    z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
  fi
  if test -z "${z_region}" || test -z "${z_project_id}"; then return 0; fi

  local -r z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
  local -r z_docker_images="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbho_probe_director_images.txt"
  local -r z_docker_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbho_probe_director_stderr.txt"

  # Collect docker images into temp file
  docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
    > "${z_docker_images}" 2>"${z_docker_stderr}" \
    || return 0

  # Load lines matching this depot's GAR prefix into array
  local z_depot_images=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      "${z_gar_prefix}"*) z_depot_images+=("${z_line}") ;;

######################################################################
# Probe governor walkthrough units — sets caller-scope z_gu1..z_gu3
# Requires: z_secrets_dir already set.
#
# Unit 1: Governor credential installed
# Unit 2: Retriever AND director credentials installed (governor created them)
# Unit 3: Functional verification — a GAR image has been pulled locally
#         (proves the full charter/knight → IAM → access chain works)

zrbho_probe_governor_units() {
  z_gu1=0; z_gu2=0; z_gu3=0

  # Unit 1: Governor credential file exists
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_gu1=1
  fi

  # Unit 2: Both retriever AND director credential files exist
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_gu2=1
  fi

  # Unit 3: Functional verification — a GAR image exists locally
  # This proves the SAs the governor created actually work (IAM grants applied).
  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then return 0; fi

  local z_project_id="" z_region=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID") || z_project_id=""
    z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
  fi
  if test -n "${z_region}" && test -n "${z_project_id}"; then
    local z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${z_gar_prefix}"; then
      z_gu3=1
    fi
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Probe payor walkthrough units — sets caller-scope z_pu1..z_pu4
# Requires: z_secrets_dir already set.
#
# Unit 1: OAuth credential present (rbro-payor.env in secrets dir)
# Unit 2: Payor project configured (RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID non-empty)
# Unit 3: Depot provisioned (RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID non-empty)
# Unit 4: Governor SA exists (governor credential file present)

zrbho_probe_payor_units() {
  z_pu1=0
  z_pu2=0
  z_pu3=0
  z_pu4=0

  # Unit 1: OAuth credential present
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/rbro-payor.env"; then
    z_pu1=1
  fi

  # Unit 2: Payor project configured
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrp_file}"; then
    local z_probe_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_probe_line; do
      case "${z_probe_line}" in RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID=?*) z_pu2=1; break ;; esac
    done < "${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
  fi

  # Unit 3: Depot provisioned
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    local z_probe_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_probe_line; do
      case "${z_probe_line}" in RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=?*) z_pu3=1; break ;; esac
    done < "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  fi

  # Unit 4: Governor SA credential file exists
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_pu4=1
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Onboarding triage — helper and entry point

# Args: detected(0|1) role_name colophon
zrbho_triage_role() {
  local -r z_detected="${1}" z_name="${2}" z_colophon="${3}"
  local -r z_url="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}#${z_name}"
  # Column pad lives OUTSIDE the link envelope so the underline/OSC-8 stop
  # at the end of the role name.  Width 13 = 12-char column + 1 separator.
  local z_pad=""
  printf -v z_pad '%*s' $((13 - ${#z_name})) ''
  if test "${z_detected}" = "1"; then
    buh_tltT " [*] " "${z_name}" "${z_url}" "${z_pad}" "${z_colophon}"
  else
    buh_tl   " [ ] " "${z_name}" "${z_url}"
  fi
}

rbho_triage() {
  # No sentinel — works pre-kindle (probes filesystem only)

  buc_doc_brief "Detect credential roles and route to per-role onboarding walkthrough"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_has_payor z_has_governor z_has_director z_has_retriever z_secrets_dir
  zrbho_probe_role_credentials

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  buh_section "Recipe Bottle Onboarding"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "  " "Recipe Bottle" "${z_docs}" " builds container images with supply-chain provenance"
  buh_t   "  and runs untrusted containers behind enforced network isolation."
  buh_e

  # Each role: detected → walkthrough tabtarget, absent → docs link
  zrbho_triage_role "${z_has_retriever}" "Retriever" "${RBZ_ONBOARD_RETRIEVER}"
  zrbho_triage_role "${z_has_director}"  "Director"  "${RBZ_ONBOARD_DIRECTOR}"
  zrbho_triage_role "${z_has_governor}"  "Governor"  "${RBZ_ONBOARD_GOVERNOR}"
  zrbho_triage_role "${z_has_payor}"     "Payor"     "${RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR}"

  buh_e
  buh_t  "  For a full health dashboard across all roles:"
  buh_tT "    " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_REFERENCE}"

}

######################################################################
# Onboarding reference — all roles, all units, single health dashboard

rbho_reference() {
  # No sentinel — works pre-kindle (probes filesystem only)

  buc_doc_brief "Reference dashboard — all roles, all units, current probe status"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_has_payor z_has_governor z_has_director z_has_retriever z_secrets_dir
  zrbho_probe_role_credentials

  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Onboarding Reference"
  buh_e
  buh_t  "  Health dashboard across all roles. Re-run anytime to check status."
  buh_e

  # Retriever — full per-unit probes
  buh_section "Retriever"
  local z_ru1 z_ru2 z_ru3 z_ru4
  zrbho_probe_retriever_units
  zrbho_po_status "${z_ru1}" "  Credential gate — SA key installed"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_ru2}" "  First artifact — hallmark summoned locally"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_ru3}" "  Container runtime — crucible charged"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_ru4}" "  Local experimentation — kludge image present"

zrbho_po_status "${z_du2}" "  Local build — kludge image present"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_du3}" "  Depot foundation — base images enshrined"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_du4}" "  Conjure — production build image present"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_du5}" "  Bind — pinned upstream image present"

  zrbho_po_status "${z_du7}" "  Full ark — all three modes compared"
  buh_tT "  Walkthrough: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_DIRECTOR}"

  # Governor — full per-unit probes
  buh_section "Governor"

  buh_tT "  Walkthrough: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_GOVERNOR}"
  buh_e

  # Payor — full per-unit probes
  buh_section "Payor"
  local z_pu1=0

  zrbho_probe_payor_units
  zrbho_po_status "${z_pu1}" "  OAuth bootstrap — credentials installed"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_pu2}" "  Project setup — GCP project configured"
  zrbho_po_status "${z_pu3}" "  Depot provisioning — infrastructure levied"

  buh_tT "  Walkthrough: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR}"
  buh_e

}

######################################################################
# Onboarding role walkthroughs — dual-mode rendering

rbho_retriever() {
  buc_doc_brief "Retriever walkthrough — pull and run vessel images"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # --- Extract config and probe ---
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi

  local z_ru1 z_ru2 z_ru3 z_ru4
  zrbho_probe_retriever_units

  # --- Count progress ---
  local z_done=0
  if test "${z_ru1}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_ru2}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_ru3}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_ru4}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  local -r z_total=4

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Retriever Walkthrough"
  buh_e

  if test "${z_done}" = "${z_total}"; then
    # ============ REFERENCE MODE — all probes green ============
    buh_t  "  All steps complete. Re-run anytime to verify health."
    buh_e
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Credential gate — SA key installed"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  First artifact — hallmark summoned locally"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Container runtime — crucible charged"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Local experimentation — kludge image present"
  else
    # ============ WALKTHROUGH MODE — show frontier unit ============
    local -r z_frontier=$((z_done + 1))
    buh_t  "  Step ${z_frontier} of ${z_total}"
    buh_e

    if test "${z_ru1}" = "0"; then
      # ---- Unit 1: Credential Gate ----
      buh_section "  Credential Gate"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  A " "depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" " is the facility where container images are built and stored."
      buh_tlt "  A " "retriever" "${z_docs}#Retriever" " is a role with read access to a depot — you pull and run"
      buh_t   "  container images that others have built."
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  To access a depot, you need a service account key. Your governor creates"
      buh_t   "  one by running:"
      buh_tT  "    " "${RBZ_CHARTER_RETRIEVER}"
      buh_e
      if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
        buh_t   "  Install the key file to:"
        buh_tc  "    " "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
      else
        buh_tW  "  " "Project not configured — .rbk/rbrr.env not found."
        buh_t   "  Run the payor walkthrough first, or ask your payor for the project files."
      fi
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  Once installed, re-run this walkthrough to continue."

    elif test "${z_ru2}" = "0"; then
      # ---- Unit 2: First Artifact ----
      buh_section "  First Artifact"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  A " "vessel" "${z_docs}#Vessel" " is a specification for a container image."
      buh_tlt "  A " "hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " is a specific build instance of a vessel, identified by"
      buh_t   "  timestamp."
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  " "Summon" "${z_docs}#Summon" " pulls a hallmark image from the depot to your local machine:"
      buh_tT  "    " "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  If something fails, check the " "Log" "${z_docs}#Log" " — the file paths are printed at the"
      buh_t   "  top of the command output."
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  After summoning, inspect the artifact's provenance:"
      buh_tT  "    " "${RBZ_PLUMB_FULL}"
      buh_tT  "    " "${RBZ_PLUMB_COMPACT}"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  A " "vouch" "${z_docs}#Vouch" " is cryptographic attestation proving the artifact was built"
      buh_t   "  by trusted infrastructure."
      buh_tlt "  " "Plumb" "${z_docs}#Plumb" " lets you inspect the SBOM, build info, and vouch chain —"
      buh_t   "  this is how you know what you're running."

    elif test "${z_ru3}" = "0"; then
      # ---- Unit 3: Container Runtime ----
      buh_section "  Container Runtime"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  A " "bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " is your workload container, running unmodified in a controlled"
      buh_t   "  network environment."
      buh_tlt "  A " "nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " ties a sentry and bottle together into a runnable unit."
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  The " "sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " enforces network policies via iptables and dnsmasq."
      buh_tlt "  The " "pentacle" "${z_docs}#Pentacle" " establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle."
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  " "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " starts the sentry/pentacle/bottle triad:"
      buh_tI  "    " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${RBCC_onboarding_nameplate}"
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  Shell into the bottle and look around:"
      buh_tTc "    " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${RBCC_onboarding_nameplate}"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  When done, " "quench" "${z_docs}#Quench" " stops and cleans up:"
      buh_tI  "    " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${RBCC_onboarding_nameplate}"

    else
      # ---- Unit 4: Local Experimentation ----
      buh_section "  Local Experimentation"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " builds a vessel image locally for fast iteration — no registry"
      buh_t   "  push, no director credentials needed:"
      buh_tT  "    " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}"
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  After kludging, charge a nameplate to test your local build, then rack in"
      buh_t   "  and look around. Kludge is the retriever's experimentation tool — iterate"
      buh_t   "  on your local environment without Cloud Build."
    fi
  fi

  buh_e
  buh_tT "  Triage: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_TRIAGE}"

}

rbho_director() {
  buc_doc_brief "Director walkthrough — build and publish vessel images"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # --- Extract config and probe ---
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi

  local z_du1=0
  local z_du2=0
  local z_du3=0
  local z_du4=0
  local z_du5=0
  local z_du6=0
  local z_du7=0
  zrbho_probe_director_units

  # --- Count progress ---
  local z_done=0
  if test "${z_du1}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_du2}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_du3}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_du4}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_du5}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_du6}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  if test "${z_du7}" = "1"; then z_done=$((z_done + 1)); fi
  local -r z_total=7

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Director Walkthrough"
  buh_e

  if test "${z_done}" = "${z_total}"; then
    # ============ REFERENCE MODE — all probes green ============
    buh_t  "  All steps complete. Re-run anytime to verify health."
    buh_e
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Credential gate — director SA key installed"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Local build — kludge image present"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Depot foundation — base images enshrined"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Conjure — production build image present"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Bind — pinned upstream image present"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Graft — locally-built image pushed"
    zrbho_po_status 1 "  Full ark — all three modes compared"
  else
    # ============ WALKTHROUGH MODE — show frontier unit ============
    local -r z_frontier=$((z_done + 1))
    buh_t  "  Step ${z_frontier} of ${z_total}"
    buh_e

    if test "${z_du1}" = "0"; then
      # ---- Unit 1: Credential Gate ----
      buh_section "  Credential Gate"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  A " "depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" " is the facility where container images are built and stored."
      buh_tlt "  A " "director" "${z_docs}#Director" " is a role with build and publish access to a depot —"
      buh_t   "  you create container images and push them to the registry."
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  Where a retriever can only pull, a director can build, push, and manage"
      buh_t   "  artifacts. Your governor knighted this service account for build operations."
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  To access a depot, you need a service account key. Your governor creates"
      buh_t   "  one by running:"
      buh_tT  "    " "${RBZ_KNIGHT_DIRECTOR}"
      buh_e
      if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
        buh_t   "  Install the key file to:"
        buh_tc  "    " "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
      else
        buh_tW  "  " "Project not configured — .rbk/rbrr.env not found."
        buh_t   "  Run the payor walkthrough first, or ask your payor for the project files."
      fi
      buh_e
      buh_t   "  Once installed, re-run this walkthrough to continue."

    elif test "${z_du2}" = "0"; then
      # ---- Unit 2: Kludge — Local Build ----
      buh_section "  Kludge: Local Build"
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  A " "vessel" "${z_docs}#Vessel" " is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile,"
      buh_t   "  build context, and metadata defining what gets built."
      buh_e
      buh_tlt "  " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no Cloud Build"
      buh_t   "  setup needed, no registry push. The fastest way to see a vessel come to life."

1147	######################################################################
1148	# Configure your Repo's Environment — universal prerequisite handbook
1149	#
1150	# Hands-on walk-through: run commands, read output, configure your
1151	# station. By the end, the learner has working local tooling and
1152	# understands tabtargets, regimes, and the render/validate pattern.
1153	
1154	rbho_crash_course() {
1155	  # No sentinel — works pre-kindle (probes filesystem only)
1156	
1157	  buc_doc_brief "Configure your Repo's Environment — tabtargets, regimes, station setup, logs"
1158	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
1159	
1160	  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"
1161	
1162	  # --- Probes ---
1163	  local z_rbrr_project="" z_rbrr_populated=0
1164	  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
1165	    z_rbrr_project=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID") || z_rbrr_project=""
1166	    test -n "${z_rbrr_project}" && z_rbrr_populated=1
1167	  fi
1168	
1169	  local z_station_present=0 z_log_dir=""
1170	  if test -n "${BURD_STATION_FILE:-}" && test -f "${BURD_STATION_FILE}"; then
1171	    z_station_present=1
1172	    z_log_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" "BURS_LOG_DIR") || z_log_dir=""
1173	  fi
1174	
1175	  # --- Header ---
1176	  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Configure your Repo's Environment"
1177	  buh_e
1178	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
1179	
1180	  # --- Step 1: What you ran to get here ---
1181	  buh_step1 "What you ran to get here"
1182	  buh_e
1183	  buh_tlt "The command you just ran is a " "Tabtarget" "${z_docs}#Tabtarget" " — a launcher script"
1184	  buh_t   "in the ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/ directory. Tab completion narrows by prefix: type \`${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/rbw-<TAB>\` to see every"
1185	  buh_tlt "" "Recipe Bottle" "${z_docs}" " command."
1186	  buh_e
1187	
1188	  # --- Step 2: View the project config (BURC) ---
1189	  buh_step1 "View the project config"
1190	  buh_e
1191	  buh_tlt "A " "Regime" "${z_docs}#Regime" " is a configuration file with a schema, a renderer,"
1192	  buh_t   "and a validator. Run the renderer for the project config regime:"
1193	  buh_e
1194	  buh_tT  "   " "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}"
1195	  buh_e
1196	  buh_tlt "" "BURC" "${z_docs}#BURC" " is checked into git — shared project settings that every"
1197	  buh_t   "clone gets. It tells the launcher where to find tools and where"
1198	  buh_t   "to look for your personal station file."
1199	  buh_e
1200	
1201	  # --- Step 3: View your station (BURS) ---
1202	  buh_step1 "View your personal station"
1203	  buh_e
1204	  buh_tlt "" "BURS" "${z_docs}#BURS" " is your per-developer station file: local, gitignored,"
1205	  buh_t   "holds things that vary per machine. Run the renderer:"
1206	  buh_e
1207	  buh_tT  "   " "${BUWZ_RS_RENDER}"
1208	  buh_e
1209	  buh_tltlt "The repo-vs-personal split is deliberate: " "BURC" "${z_docs}#BURC" " travels with the code; " "BURS" "${z_docs}#BURS" " stays on your machine."
1210	  buh_e
1211	
1212	  # --- Step 4: Validate your station ---
1213	  buh_step1 "Validate your station"
1214	  buh_e
1215	  buh_tlt "Every " "Regime" "${z_docs}#Regime" " has a validate tabtarget that checks the file against"
1216	  buh_t   "its schema. This may fail if your station file is missing fields"
1217	  buh_t   "beyond the minimum the launcher required — that is expected."
1218	  buh_t   "Run it:"
1219	  buh_e
1220	  buh_tT  "   " "${BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE}"
1221	  buh_e
1222	  buh_t   "Read the error if it fails — it names the field and tells you"
1223	  buh_t   "what to fill in."
1224	  buh_e
1225	  if test "${z_station_present}" = "1"; then
1226	    buh_tctct "" " [*] " " Station file present at " "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" ""
1227	  else
1228	    zrbho_po_status 0 "Station file not found"
1229	  fi
1230	  buh_e
1231	
1232	  # --- Step 5: Validate the repo regime ---
1233	  buh_step1 "Validate the repo regime"
1234	  buh_e
1235	  buh_tlt "The repository regime (" "RBRR" "${z_docs}#RBRR" ") holds your team's " "Depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" ""
1236	  buh_t   "identity — the GCP project where container images are built and stored."
1237	  buh_t   "Run the validator:"
1238	  buh_e
1239	  buh_tT  "   " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO}"
1240	  buh_e
1241	  buh_tlt "On a bare fork, " "RBRR" "${z_docs}#RBRR" " fields are blank and validation will fail —"
1242	  buh_tltlt "you need a " "Payor" "${z_docs}#Payor" " account and a " "Depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" " to populate them."
1243	  buh_t   "On a team repo, they are already populated and validation passes."
1244	  buh_t   "Either way, read the output — it tells you exactly what state you're in."
1245	  buh_e
1246	  if test "${z_rbrr_populated}" = "1"; then
1247	    zrbho_po_status 1 "RBRR populated — depot project: ${z_rbrr_project}"
1248	  else
1249	    zrbho_po_status 0 "RBRR not populated — depot identity fields are blank"
1250	  fi
1251	  buh_e
1252	
1253	  # --- Step 6: Check your logs ---
1254	  buh_step1 "Check your logs"
1255	  buh_e
1256	  buh_t   "When you ran the validator, it printed file paths at the top"
1257	  buh_tlt "of its output. Every state-changing command writes three " "Log" "${z_docs}#Log" ""
1258	  buh_tlt "files to " "BURS" "${z_docs}#BURS" "_LOG_DIR:"
1259	  buh_e
1260	  if test -n "${z_log_dir}"; then
1261	    buh_tct "   stable    " "${z_log_dir}/${BURC_LOG_LAST}.${BURC_LOG_EXT}" "  (always the same path, great for Claude)"
1262	  else
1263	    buh_t   "   stable    always the same path — tooling reads this one"
1264	  fi
1265	  buh_t   "   per-cmd   same filename across runs — diff between executions"
1266	  buh_t   "   history   timestamped — permanent record, never overwritten"
1267	  buh_e
1268	  buh_tlt "Some commands also write a " "Transcript" "${z_docs}#Transcript" " — a single file"
1269	  buh_t   "capturing key decision points and state transitions. When a"
1270	  buh_t   "command fails, the transcript is the first thing to read."
1271	  buh_e
1272	  buh_t   "Handbook display commands (like this one) do not log — teaching"
1273	  buh_t   "output is ephemeral by design."
1274	  buh_e
1275	
1276	  # --- Step 7: The pattern ---
1277	  buh_step1 "The pattern"
1278	  buh_e
1279	  buh_tlt "Every " "Regime" "${z_docs}#Regime" " has a render and a validate tabtarget."
1280	  buh_t   "The letter after \`r\` is all that changes:"
1281	  buh_e
1282	  buh_tltTtT "   c  " "BURC" "${z_docs}#BURC" "  " "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}" "   " "${BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE}" ""
1283	  buh_tltTtT "   s  " "BURS" "${z_docs}#BURS" "  " "${BUWZ_RS_RENDER}" "  " "${BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE}" ""
1284	  buh_tltTtT "   r  " "RBRR" "${z_docs}#RBRR" "  " "${RBZ_RENDER_REPO}" "     " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO}" ""
1285	  buh_tltTtT "   p  " "RBRP" "${z_docs}#RBRP" "  " "${RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR}" "    " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR}" ""
1286	  buh_tltTtT "   o  " "RBRO" "${z_docs}#RBRO" "  " "${RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH}" "    " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH}" ""
1287	  buh_e
1288	  buh_t   "These take a target name (vessel, nameplate, or role):"
1289	  buh_e
1290	  buh_tltTtT "   v  " "RBRV" "${z_docs}#RBRV" "  " "${RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL}" "     " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL}" ""
1291	  buh_tltTtT "   n  " "RBRN" "${z_docs}#RBRN" "  " "${RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE}" "  " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE}" ""
1292	  buh_tltTtT "   a  " "RBRA" "${z_docs}#RBRA" "  " "${RBZ_RENDER_AUTH}" "       " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}" ""
1293	  buh_e
1294	  buh_t   "Learn the letter — you can find any regime's tools from it."
1295	  buh_e
1296	
1297	  # --- Step 8: Next steps ---
1298	  buh_step1 "Next steps"
1299	  buh_e
1300	  buh_t  "Your repo environment is configured. The tools work, errors explain"
1301	  buh_tlt "themselves, and " "Logs" "${z_docs}#Log" " land where you told them to."
1302	  buh_e
1303	  buh_t  "Return to the start menu for what to do next:"
1304	  buh_tT "   " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
1305	  buh_e
1306	}
1307	
1308	######################################################################
1309	# Credential installation — shared utility for retriever/director
1310	#
1311	# Args: role_display  role_constant  knight_tabtarget_constant  role_description
1312	# Probes RBRR for secrets dir, checks credential file, shows install
1313	# path, points to auth regime validator.
1314	
1315	zrbho_credential_install() {
1316	  local -r z_role_constant="${1}"
1317	  local -r z_knight_constant="${2}"
1318	  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"
1319	
1320	  # --- Probes ---
1321	  local z_secrets_dir=""
1322	  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
1323	    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
1324	  fi
1325	
1326	  local z_cred_present=0
1327	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" \
1328	     && test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_role_constant}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
1329	    z_cred_present=1
1330	  fi
1331	
1332	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
1333	
1334	  # --- Step 1: Get the key file ---
1335	  buh_step1 "Get the key file"
1336	  buh_e
1337	  buh_tlt "Your " "Governor" "${z_docs}#Governor" " creates a service account key by running:"
1338	  buh_e
1339	  buh_tT  "   " "${z_knight_constant}"
1340	  buh_e
1341	  buh_tlt "The output is an " "RBRA" "${z_docs}#RBRA" " credential file. The governor hands"
1342	  buh_t   "it to you out-of-band — this is a secret, not committed to the repo."
1343	  buh_e
1344	
1345	  # --- Step 2: Install the key file ---
1346	  buh_step1 "Install the key file"
1347	  buh_e
1348	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
1349	    buh_tlt "Place the file at the path derived from " "RBRR" "${z_docs}#RBRR" ":"
1350	    buh_e
1351	    buh_c   "   ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_role_constant}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
1352	    buh_e
1353	    buh_t   "Create the directory if it does not exist."
1354	  else
1355	    buh_tW  "" "RBRR not populated — cannot determine credential path."
1356	    buh_tlt "Run " "Configure your Repo's Environment" "${z_docs}#BURC" " first."
1357	  fi
1358	  buh_e
1359	  if test "${z_cred_present}" = "1"; then
1360	    zrbho_po_status 1 "Credential file present"
1361	  else
1362	    zrbho_po_status 0 "Credential file not found"
1363	  fi
1364	  buh_e
1365	
1366	  # --- Step 3: Validate ---
1367	  buh_step1 "Validate"
1368	  buh_e
1369	  buh_tlt "Run the " "RBRA" "${z_docs}#RBRA" " validator for your role:"
1370	  buh_e
1371	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}" " ${z_role_constant}"
1372	  buh_e
1373	  buh_t   "Read the output — it checks the file format and reports"
1374	  buh_t   "what the credential grants."
1375	  buh_e
1376	
1377	  # --- Step 4: Next steps ---
1378	  buh_step1 "Next steps"
1379	  buh_e
1380	  buh_t  "Return to the start menu:"
1381	  buh_tT "   " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
1382	  buh_e
1383	}
1384	
1385	######################################################################
1386	# Install Retriever Credentials
1387	
1388	rbho_credential_retriever() {
1389	  buc_doc_brief "Install retriever credentials — place RBRA key, validate, confirm pull access"
1390	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
1391	  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"
1392	
1393	  buh_section "Install Retriever Credentials"
1394	  buh_e
1395	  buh_tlt "A " "Retriever" "${z_docs}#Retriever" " pulls container images from the"
1396	  buh_tlt "  " "Depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" " — read-only access to what others have built."
1397	  buh_e

1400	}

1402	######################################################################
1403	# Install Director Credentials

1405	rbho_credential_director() {

1414	  buh_e

1417	}
